Summer HVAC Maintenance Tips To Make Sure You Keep Cool

Summer HVAC Maintenance Tips To Make Sure You Keep Cool

Publish Date : 2025-02-18
Views : 1200
(760) 392-7616 Schedule An Appointment

Finding a reputable service company doesn't have to be as difficult as dealing with a broken air conditioner. But nowadays, you don’t worry about it because you can easily find HVAC technicians in Riverside for repair services. They are highly experienced and qualified with the capacity to offer high-quality results on time. A professional HVAC air conditioner repair crew stays updated with the latest air conditioning technologies and local building requirements to be your reliable partner. By putting that knowledge to work for you, they can accomplish most tasks quickly while still paying attention to the nuances.

Professional HVAC repair services in Riverside include various services such as.

  • Tune-ups and diagnostics,
  • Upgrades and installation,
  • Cleaning and upkeep of the system and putting an end to strange noises or vibrations,
  • Eliminating refrigerant leaks,
  • Units' power is being restored,
  • Evaporator coils need to be replaced,
  • Fan motors that have failed and require repair or replacement,
  • Defective compressors are repaired and replaced,
  • Plugged condensate drains.

You can avail any of the above heating and air conditioning repair services in Riverside when you need them. Besides this, licensed technicians can provide emergency services 24 hours a day, seven days a week, and in addition to it, you can get an appointment on weekends and public holidays.

Maintenance Tips For Efficient Working Of Your HVAC System

The warmest time of the year has arrived, bringing with it an increased danger of heatstroke. For newborns, the elderly, and individuals with specific medical issues, extreme heat inside a home can be unbearable. Your HVAC system may struggle to keep up as the summer heat rises. If you want your HVAC system to run at top efficiency, you'll need to pay some care. Let’s have a look at the maintenance tips if your HVAC system is not working properly:

1. Examination Of The Filter

If you don't change your filter from time to time, you need to do it now. The frequency with which you should update your filter is determined by your unique circumstances.

Do you have several pets or live in a dusty or polluted environment? After that, you might want to change it every three weeks. If you don't use your AC very frequently or don't have any allergies, you can replace it for a longer duration.

It's crucial to change the filter daily, as it protects your HVAC system and your health from debris and dust. The filter of the HVAC system catches airborne pollutants, avoiding them from circulating in your home.

2. Make Sure The Condenser Coils Are Clean.

Turn off your condenser's electricity. After that, spray the coils with water to get rid of any dirt or debris. The cooling efficiency will get reduced if there is a dirty condenser coil, and it may struggle to transmit heat to the outside air.

3. Air Ducts That Aren't Filtered.

Debris will accrue inside the home's ductwork over the period. The airflow of the AC will be restricted if ductwork is blocked with too much debris and dust. You can also hire a professional for this task. Check with your local air conditioning and heating repair firm to see whether they do preventative HVAC maintenance.

4. Make Sure The Vents Aren't Clogged.

You should avoid the use of heavy pieces of furniture blocking any openings in your house if you want to get the most out of the AC. The placement of heavy furniture in front of a vent can obstruct the passage of air into your home. If a vent is clogged, it starts straining on the AC causing it to struggle and maintain the right temperature in the home.

5. Examine The Electrical System.

Even you need to check an HVAC system's wiring requirements now and again. Wires decay and corrode as a result of exposure or overheating at connector ends. Call an HVAC technician in Riverside if you notice any scorched wire.

6. Check The Refrigerant Levels And Re-fill Them If Necessary.

Your air conditioner uses refrigerant to chill your home. Because of its frequent use, this refrigerant will ultimately run out. There are also sometimes leaks in the line that causes it to empty more quickly.

7. Check The Thermostat For Errors

The HVAC system can break down occasionally due to a faulty thermostat. It may be unable to detect your home's temperature and hence fail to switch on to cool it down. A skilled expert can easily fix your thermostat and ensure that it is properly standardized.

8. Remove The Obstruction From The Condensed Drain Tube

When the AC's drain tube becomes clogged, water leaks into the furnace. You might be speculating how the condensate drain tube gets clogged. The bacteria appear on the drain to clog the drain tube over time. It's ideal to connect with professionals to clean the drain tube and drain pan.

9. Examine The Components And Wiring.

Turn off the AC unit before beginning this approach and remove the access panel from the condensing unit. Examine the insulation wires for signs of melted, blackened, or scorched insulation. Ensure that the electrical connections are in good working order. You can check the capacitors by using an electrical test meter in the unit if you have any.

Furthermore, if the contactor switch shows signs of severe pitting, it should be replaced. If you're not confident in your ability to replace the parts yourself, contact a heating and cooling professional.

10. Examination Of The Thermostat

Make sure your thermostat is set to the correct temperature across your home, and you also cross-check it from time to time. If you have an older or mechanical thermostat, consider upgrading to a programmed thermostat. A programmed thermostat automatically turns off your air conditioner when no one is at home.

11. Checking The Condenser Unit Of The Fan

Examine the fan mounted on the condenser after the power is turned off. If one or more of the fan blades show signs of cracking, replace them. Oil the fan motor bearings on older air conditioners regularly.

12. Get Yourself A Programmable Thermostat.

With a programmable thermostat, you can easily set up and regulate temperatures at different times of the day. You may even program the thermostat to adjust the temperature automatically to save money on your electricity bill. As a result, keeping the cooling impact of your air conditioner without having to keep your hand on the dial is simple. You can also install a thermostat by following the user manual's instructions. But still, if you are facing any kind of issue, you can opt for HVAC repair services in Riverside.

13. Replace A Sagging Air Conditioner Pad In Your Home

Checking of a strain on the electric wires and copper coolant tubes. Water puddles will form around the AC unit if the conditioner pad sinks. Aside from that, concrete pads beneath heat exchanger units tend to sink with time, affecting the AC's temperature. So, if you are suffering from this situation, then take immediate action to resolve it. You can hire HVAC technicians in Riverside for installing a board under the air conditioner. By doing so, you can raise the pad according to your requirement.

Some maintenance activities can be completed by a layperson, while others require the assistance of a licensed professional. You'll find a welcoming home waiting for you. If you follow these AC maintenance suggestions, your system will work smoothly and in good working order.

Conclusion

It is crucial to note that the air conditioning pad does not need to be removed from the air conditioning machine. A little bit of summer HVAC maintenance can go a long way. It's frequently the finest approach to catch a minor issue before it becomes a major one. If you're having issues with your HVAC system, you can trust expert technicians in Riverside to take care of it.

You can book an appointment with EZ Heat and Air for accurate solutions. A simple check-up regularly will help you avoid having to replace your air conditioner or any of its parts. A well-maintained air conditioner consumes less energy and cools a room quickly. Furthermore, the machine will last longer and have fewer risks of breaking down. You can easily save money on the electricity bills because of the use of less power by the AC.
